name: Tokkuri Tei
tagline: Honolulu's original izakaya — the garlic ahi belly and tofu steak are why reservations fill a week out.
mustOrder: ika yaki (squid pancake, $12–14 — THE signature dish)
parking: Street parking on side streets mauka of Kapahulu
talkStory: Opened in the early 1990s by chef/owner Hide Yoshimoto (Hide-san), Tokkuri Tei was one of the first authentic izakayas in Hawaii and helped spark the island's entire izakaya boom. The ika yaki (squid pancake) is the legendary signature dish. Order multiple small plates to share — izakaya style means rounds of dishes, not one entree per person. Ask staff for sake pairing guidance. Go Tue/Wed for walk-in seating; weekends book up fast.
